headline: Your waterfall campaign journey.
subheadline: A scrollable GTMx strategy artifact for routing every qualified account into the right AI-agent offer.
productSummary: An enterprise Agentic Workforce Management platform that lets organizations hire, train, deploy, and orchestrate pre-trained AI agents, with Prime AI as the central overseer.
coreInsight: NextGen sells AI agents that do the work companies currently hire humans to do. The cleanest buying signal is therefore open job requisitions for automatable roles.

sections.label: Signal
sections.title: The signal that proves budget
sections.takeaway: Open reqs are not vague intent. They show approved budget, active pain, and the exact role an AI agent can replace or augment.

sections.label: Waterfall
sections.title: One cascade routes the entire TAM
sections.takeaway: Every account exits with exactly one tier. Strongest hiring intent wins first, and the catch-all keeps coverage at 100%.

sections.label: Tiers
sections.title: Each tier gets a different offer
sections.takeaway: The campaign is personalized by trigger intensity: team build-out, multi-function, single seat, orphaned AI, or future-proof readiness.

sections.label: Campaigns
sections.title: Campaign angles, grouped for action
sections.takeaway: The waterfall tiers carry the volume. Side campaigns stay optional, focused, and time-boxed.

sections.label: Email Copy
sections.title: Copy that asks for a tiny yes
sections.takeaway: Each sequence uses the same arc: notice the signal, name the hiring drag, offer the magnet, and make the reply frictionless.

sections.label: Launch
sections.title: Launch narrow, then scale what replies
sections.takeaway: Start with Tier 1, prove reply density, then roll the winning language into the wider waterfall.

signalReasons: Reliably detectable from LinkedIn Jobs, Indeed, careers pages, and Apollo.
Carries budget because an open requisition means money is already approved.
Maps directly to NextGen copy: your next hire does not have to be human.
Signal intensity creates the priority ladder for the campaign tiers.

sources.name: LinkedIn Sales Nav + Jobs
sources.delivers: TAM, open reqs, building-team language, decision-maker titles

sources.name: Indeed / job-board scrape
sources.delivers: SMB and mid-market backfill, role titles, posting recency

sources.name: Apollo
sources.delivers: Firmographics, contact data, department headcount, job signals

sources.name: Company careers scrape
sources.delivers: Authoritative live req counts when job boards lag

sources.name: BuiltWith / Wappalyzer
sources.delivers: Point AI tool detection for the orphaned AI tier

routingRules.if: in TAM, no reqs, no AI tool

tiers.name: Tier 1
tiers.label: Team Build-Out
tiers.trigger: 4+ open automatable reqs, or explicit scaling language
tiers.pain: Approved budget to hire a team for repetitive work, with months of recruiting, ramp, turnover, and overhead ahead.
tiers.valueProp: Deploy a full agentic workforce in days, not months. One named agent per function, orchestrated by Prime AI.

tiers.name: Tier 2
tiers.label: Multi-Function
tiers.pain: Scaling several repetitive functions at once while the ops leader is stretched across multiple priorities.
tiers.valueProp: Deploy 2-3 specialized agents that never clock out and can go live in days.

tiers.name: Tier 3
tiers.label: Single Seat
tiers.pain: The automate-versus-hire question is live right now for one repetitive role.
tiers.valueProp: Fill the seat with an agent that works 24/7 at a fraction of loaded cost.

tiers.name: Tier 4
tiers.label: Orphaned AI
tiers.trigger: No open reqs, but a point AI tool is detected
tiers.pain: They started automating, but their tools are scattered and hard to orchestrate across departments.
tiers.valueProp: Prime AI becomes the command center for the whole AI workforce instead of a drawer of disconnected bots.

tiers.name: Catch-all
tiers.label: Future-Proof
tiers.trigger: In TAM, no reqs, no AI tool
tiers.pain: They have not started automating yet, while competitors quietly cut labor cost and run 24/7.
tiers.valueProp: Start with one agent before the next hire. Reduce cost, respond faster, and scale smarter.

campaigns.name: Future-Proof
campaigns.filter: In TAM, 0 open reqs, no AI tool detected
campaigns.strategy: Generate 1 tailored use case from website + industry
campaigns.overview: A soft catch-all angle showing where NextGen would put the first agent to work.

campaigns.name: Single Seat
campaigns.strategy: Pull exact job title + name the matching agent
campaigns.overview: Frame the open role as a live automate-before-you-hire decision.

campaigns.name: Multi-Function
campaigns.strategy: Map each role to a named agent
campaigns.overview: Show how 2-3 specialized agents cover the functions they are hiring for.

campaigns.name: Team Build-Out
campaigns.strategy: Count reqs and quantify hiring drag
campaigns.overview: The flagship blueprint mapping open roles to agents and 12-month cost delta.

campaigns.name: Orphaned AI / No Command Center
campaigns.strategy: Detect the tool and contrast with orchestration
campaigns.overview: Reframe scattered bots into a Prime AI command-center opportunity.

campaigns.name: Creative Ideas
campaigns.strategy: Website analysis into 3 specific agent use cases
campaigns.overview: A useful curiosity-led variant: I had an idea for your company.

campaigns.name: New Ops Leader Welcome
campaigns.filter: New COO / VP Ops / Head of CX under 90 days
campaigns.strategy: Pull start date and infer the inherited mandate
campaigns.overview: Position one agent as a fast 90-day win for the new leader.

campaigns.name: CareChoice Lookalike
campaigns.strategy: Reference CareChoice and coordination load
campaigns.overview: Use the 2M+ care-hours proof point with HIPAA-ready positioning.

campaigns.name: Marco Lookalike
campaigns.strategy: Reference Marco's 2.8M devices analyzed
campaigns.overview: Translate machine-scale repetitive analysis into their support or device workflows.

campaigns.name: COE Distribution Lookalike
campaigns.strategy: Reference COE's 30% growth
campaigns.overview: Use growth pressure as the reason to absorb ops load without linear hiring.

campaigns.name: Regulated-Industry Compliance-First
campaigns.strategy: Detect regulated vertical and lead with compliance
campaigns.overview: Overlay SOC 2, GDPR, HIPAA, and FINRA-ready language on any tier.

campaigns.name: BPO / Outsourcing Replacement
campaigns.filter: Visible offshore/BPO support or large ops teams
campaigns.strategy: Detect support-team size or BPO mentions
campaigns.overview: Position agents as a 24/7 replacement or augmentation layer for repetitive BPO work.

campaigns.name: Social AI Suite
campaigns.filter: Hiring social media manager / content coordinator
campaigns.strategy: Detect open social req or thin social headcount
campaigns.overview: Product-specific pitch for always-on content and insights.

campaigns.name: AI eCommerce
campaigns.filter: Shopify/BigCommerce retailers hiring catalog or CX ops
campaigns.strategy: Detect eCommerce stack + open ops req
campaigns.overview: Pitch catalog, CX, and merchandising operations on autopilot.

campaigns.name: eCommerce Peak-Season Prep
campaigns.strategy: Detect eComm + seasonal hiring spikes
campaigns.overview: Handle Black Friday support and order volume with agents instead of seasonal temps.

campaigns.name: Funding Trigger
campaigns.strategy: Crunchbase round + ops scale inference
campaigns.overview: Scale operations with agents instead of a post-raise hiring spree.

campaigns.name: Hiring-Velocity Spike
campaigns.filter: Sharp increase in total open reqs QoQ
campaigns.strategy: Detect hiring acceleration
campaigns.overview: Quantify the spike and show the headcount they may not need.

campaigns.name: Team-Count Name-Drop
campaigns.strategy: Count and name team members
campaigns.overview: Show that agents take the repetitive portion so humans handle exceptions.

campaigns.name: Head of AI / Automation
campaigns.filter: Head of AI, Automation, or Transformation title
campaigns.strategy: Detect the champion title
campaigns.overview: Pitch Prime AI to the person chartered with automation.

campaigns.name: Event Follow-Up
campaigns.filter: AI, ops, retail, logistics event lists
campaigns.strategy: Scrape attendee/exhibitor lists
campaigns.overview: Use NextGen's Access TCA context to run warm event follow-ups.

campaigns.name: AI-Curious Execs
campaigns.strategy: Detect AI-topic engagement
campaigns.overview: Lead with consultancy as a low-commitment entry into the platform.

messages.subject: {{req_count}} open roles
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, saw {{company_name}} has {{req_count}} open reqs right now, across {{open_role}} and a few other repetitive seats.

But would it be fair to assume you're staring down months of recruiting, ramp, and overhead for work that mostly repeats itself?

We build AI agents that do that work 24/7, deployed in days, not months. Your next hire doesn't have to be human.

CareChoice ran 2M+ hours of care in 2025 without scaling their coordination team.

Reply "blueprint" and I'll map your open roles to specific agents, plus the 12-month cost delta. No call needed.

messages.subject: threaded follow-up
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, quick math. {{req_count}} hires at loaded cost is a six-figure line item before anyone even ramps.

Agents carry no salary, no benefits, no attrition, and they don't clock out. Same output, a fraction of the cost.

COE Distributing grew 30% recently without the headcount that kind of growth usually forces.

Want the blueprint? It shows the cost delta on your exact roles. Just reply yes.

messages.subject: quick question
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, no need for a call. If cutting the hiring drag on those {{req_count}} roles is worth 5 minutes, I'll send over the AI Workforce Blueprint built for {{company_name}}.

Just reply yes. Should I send it to you, or is someone else on your ops team the right person?

messages.subject: {{open_role}} + more
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, noticed {{company_name}} is hiring for {{open_role}} and a couple of other repetitive roles at once.

But would it be fair to assume your team is stretched trying to scale two or three functions at the same time?

We deploy specialized AI agents, one per function, that run 24/7 with no burnout. Live in days, not months.

Marco analyzed 2.8M+ devices with agents carrying the repetitive load.

Reply "blueprint" and I'll map each open role to the agent that covers it. No call needed.

messages.subject: threaded follow-up
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, the real cost of hiring for {{open_role}} isn't the salary. It's the 6 weeks of sourcing, interviews, and ramp before anyone is productive.

{{named_agent}} is live in days and never needs onboarding twice. Spin up the next function the same week.

Want the scoped blueprint for your open roles? Reply yes.

messages.subject: quick question
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, no call needed. I'll send a short blueprint showing which agent covers each of your open roles and what it replaces.

Just reply yes. Are you the right person, or should I send it to whoever owns hiring for {{open_role}}?

messages.subject: your {{open_role}} role
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, saw {{company_name}} is hiring a {{open_role}}.

But would it be fair to assume that's a lot of repetitive work about to land on one new person's plate?

Before you fill it, worth knowing: {{named_agent}} handles that same work 24/7, live in days, at a fraction of the loaded cost. Your next hire doesn't have to be human.

Reply "teardown" and I'll send a one-page ROI breakdown for that exact role. No call needed.

messages.subject: threaded follow-up
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, a {{open_role}} hire is roughly a year of salary, benefits, and ramp before you even know it worked.

An agent is live this week, costs a fraction, and doesn't quit. You can always add a human later for the judgment calls.

Want the ROI teardown on that role? Reply yes.

messages.subject: quick question
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, no call needed. I'll send a one-pager on what a {{open_role}} agent would cost and cover versus the hire.

Just reply yes. Should I send it to you, or whoever owns that req?

messages.subject: your {{ai_tool}} setup
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, noticed {{company_name}} is already running {{ai_tool}}.

But would it be fair to assume it's one bot doing one thing, with no way to see it or scale it across the rest of your ops?

That's the gap we close. Prime AI is a command center that runs your whole AI workforce from one hub, not a drawer of disconnected tools.

Marco coordinated agents to analyze 2.8M+ devices from one place.

Reply "audit" and I'll map your current tools under one overseer. No call needed.

messages.subject: threaded follow-up
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, most teams that start with a tool like {{ai_tool}} stall at one use case. It works, but nothing connects, so it never compounds.

We orchestrate agents across support, sales, and ops from a single command center, so automation scales instead of stalling.

Want the orchestration audit on {{company_name}}? Reply yes.

messages.subject: quick question
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, no call needed. I'll put together a short audit showing how {{ai_tool}} and your other bots would run under one command center.

Just reply yes. Should I send it to you, or whoever owns automation at {{company_name}}?

messages.subject: {{industry}} + AI
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, been looking at how teams in {{industry}} handle their repetitive back-office work.

But would it be fair to assume most of it at {{company_name}} still runs on people doing the same tasks over and over?

That's exactly what AI agents are for. They run 24/7, no overhead, no burnout, and deploy in days. The future of work has clocked in.

CareChoice ran 2M+ hours of care in 2025 without scaling their team.

Reply "scorecard" and I'll send a quick AI readiness check for {{company_name}}. No call needed.

messages.subject: threaded follow-up
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, most {{industry}} teams are carrying labor cost on work that repeats every single day. That's the first thing an agent takes off your plate.

No salary, no benefits, no attrition, running around the clock.

Want the readiness scorecard? It shows where {{company_name}} would save first. Reply yes.

messages.subject: quick question
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, no call needed. I'll send a short scorecard on where {{company_name}} could put its first AI agent to work.

Just reply yes. Should I send it to you, or is someone else the right person for this?

launchPlan: Launch Tier 1 first. It is the smallest, highest-intent segment and the strongest blueprint offer.
Phase in Tier 4 second. The Prime AI orchestration angle is differentiated and easy to explain.
Then add Tiers 2 and 3. They reuse the blueprint offer, scoped down.
Launch the catch-all last after higher-intent tiers prove the best value-prop language.
Per tier, start with a chaos test, then move to one-variable tests once a winner emerges.

thresholds.action: Cut the tier and rework trigger or offer.
